models.note: KonneticHD fast-action flagship that replaced the ONE/Method; succeeded by the R8 Core.
Revolution 8 flagship single-hand series that replaced the X, with a more fishable feel and Two-Way Feedback Loop blank.
Ultra-fast, high-line-speed single-hand series built for distance and casting into wind.
Mid-priced fast-action all-water rod on Konnetic Technology covering dry fly through streamers.
Affordable USA-built dedicated saltwater rod (Graphite IIIe); later superseded by the Maverick.
Presentation-focused dry-fly trout rod (Konnetic HD); a modern revival of Sage's classic Light Line rods.
Premium dedicated saltwater rod, each weight purpose-built for a fishery; replaced by the Salt R8 in 2023.
Current flagship saltwater series on R8 graphite — more pulling power than the Salt HD plus a refined flats taper.
Mid-priced fast-action saltwater rod for quick-shot presentations, slotting below the flagship Salt series.
Short, powerful big-fly rod tuned for heavy sinking lines and oversized streamers in fresh and salt water.
Mid-price fast-action all-water workhorse on Graphite IIIe; replaced the Response and was succeeded by the Sonic.
Premium fast-action KonneticHD small-water rod for delicate presentations and tight loops on brushy creeks and small still waters.
Purpose-built tight-line / Euro-nymphing rod with a soft, sensitive tip and low balance point from its down-locking seat.
Light trout-spey family for swinging soft-hackles and small streamers with 150–400gr heads.
Lighter, thinner-profile mid-priced two-hander covering Scandi-to-Skagit swing scenarios.
Switch range pairing with the Sonic Spey rods for tighter, smaller-water swing work.
Sage's most powerful, fastest-action two-hander for long lines and heavy winter Skagit setups.
Ultra-fast switch range for tight-quarters swing water from summer steelhead to big winter runs.
KonneticHD all-around spey family spanning Scandi-to-Skagit grain windows; discontinued and replaced by R8.
11' switch range for beach, big trout and steelhead; discontinued with the X series.
Graphite IIIe value-priced fast-action spey family for steelhead and salmon; discontinued.
Value-priced switch range including a 3-weight trout-spey option; discontinued.
Sage's value, made-in-USA series on a Graphite IIIe blank with a fast all-water action.
The distance/power taper of the 2024 4th-gen Helios redesign, built for line speed, lifting power and wind-cutting accuracy.
The finesse taper of the 2024 4th-gen Helios redesign, tuned for sensitivity, delicate presentations and tippet protection.
The distance/accuracy taper of the Helios 3 generation, a fast-action powerhouse for shooting line and fighting wind.
The finesse taper of the Helios 3 generation, prioritizing feel, delicate presentation and accuracy.
Helios-derived all-water performance rod at a mid price; freshwater lineup spans trout to bass/musky and includes Euro-nymphing 10-footers.
Dedicated saltwater build of the Recon for flats, inshore and bluewater work, in six 9-foot models 6–11wt.
S-2 fiberglass small-stream specialist with a slow-to-medium glass action for delicate close-range presentation.
Sold today as the Superfine Graphite: a slow-to-moderate lower-modulus presentation rod for spring creeks and small trout/panfish.
Entry-level value all-water rod sold primarily as a complete outfit for new anglers and as an inexpensive spare.
Orvis's flagship value freshwater single-hand trout series; the 9' 5-weight is the most popular all-around model.
Travel-packable 6-piece versions of the core freshwater Clearwater, breaking down to a 22.5" tube.
Long, light-tipped single-hand nymphing rods for European tightline tactics; 10' 2wt and 3wt are the dedicated euro models.
Faster, more powerful big-game and saltwater models (7–12wt) with saltwater reel seats and Cordura tubes.
Short two-handed switch rods (11', 6–8wt) for both overhead and Spey casting.
Light two-handed trout-Spey rods (11'4", 3–4wt) for swinging soft hackles and wets.
Full-length two-handed Spey rods (12'–13'6", 6–8wt) for swinging flies to steelhead and salmon.
Blacked-out competition/euro-nymphing edition of the Helios 3, a single long ultra-sensitive 3-weight for technical drift control.
Lightest two-handed Mission models for swinging small flies and light tippets to trout.
Eleven-foot two-handed switch rods for indicator rigs and short-swinging streamers.
Twelve-foot short Spey models with a compact swing platform and more power than the trout-Spey rods.
Longest two-handed Mission models for full Spey casting and maximum distance on big water.
Mid-curve/medium-fast (MC/MF) 3M Powerlux composite series; freshwater models run #3–#9 with dedicated #10–#12 saltwater Flatsman models. Codes read weight-length-pieces (896-4 = 8wt 9'6" 4pc).
Heptagonal carbon blank with titanium guides and AAA cork; offered in medium-fast and fast actions for tighter loops and fast recovery.

Value single-hand range with a cool-grey graphite blank and embossed-Q reel seat; the #4–#7 9' models are medium-fast and the #8–#10 predator models are fast, joined by longer euro-nymph 10' and 10'6" models.
Carbon DCT (Dynamic Core Technology) single-hand workhorse sold in Medium (M), Medium-Fast (MF) and Fast (F) actions; the action letter is carried in each model code.
Long 11' single-hand rod with a progressive medium action built to cast bombers and big dry flies for salmon and large trout, giving reach and line control through runs.
Short single-hand rod optimised for sensitive fishing on small, skinny streams for trout and grayling (discreet brown finish); distinct from the Opti Creek fly line.
All-round trout and grayling single-hand rod for delicate close presentation and long controlled casts; placed 2nd in Yellowstone Angler's 2016 5-wt shootout. Distinct from the Opti Stream fly line.
Short-lived 9' 5-weight river nymphing rod made alongside the Opti Stream and quickly superseded by it.
Fast Cross Weave carbon coastal rod with a hexagonal X-Grip, built to cut through wind and reach distant rips for sea trout.
Deep-actioned 5-piece #7 single-hander with a hexagonal X-Grip, built for long underhand casts to salmon and sea trout in tight flowing water.
Stillwater/lake single-hand rod with a removable fighting butt for playing big trout on reservoirs (distinct from the identically named Opti Stillwater fly line).
Cross Weave 5-piece double-hand salmon/spey rod with Loop's Balanced Reel Seat; later succeeded by the Opti NXT.
Cross Weave switch rod for single- or double-hand casting; codes encode weight and length (8107 = 8wt 10'7").
Medium-fast 4-piece double-hand successor to the Opti Power Spey; codes encode weight and length (9132-4 = 9wt 13'2" 4pc).
Medium-fast 4-piece switch rod in the Opti NXT family for single- or double-hand casting.
Value-priced reel with a die-cast frame but the same sealed SCS carbon drag as the machined Spectrums.
The standard fully-machined large-arbor Spectrum with sealed SCS drag, now discontinued.
Premium fully-machined Spectrum with a fully sealed SCS carbon drag and 40 detented settings for demanding fresh and saltwater duty.
Lightweight large-arbor machined Spectrum bringing the sealed SCS drag to an ultra-light rigid frame.
Fully-machined, fully-sealed big-game saltwater reel for flats and bluewater, with ~50% more max drag than the Spectrum Max it replaced.
Full-cage trout reel pairing a light classic profile with Sage's One Revolution sealed carbon drag.
Classic large-arbor click-and-pawl reel for light-line and small-stream trout fishing.
Extra-large-arbor, narrow-spool reel for ultra-fast line pickup, using a sealed carbon drag.
European nymphing reel with an ultra-thin large-diameter arbor and interchangeable balance weights to tune long Euro rods.
Large-arbor full-cage all-water reel that brought Sage's sealed carbon drag to a more accessible price point.
Multipurpose fully-machined 6061 reel spanning trout to tarpon, with a size-tuned sealed carbon SCS drag and a narrow large-arbor spool.
Lightweight version of the Shift with a cold-forged frame and spool and the sealed carbon SCS drag, in sizes 3/4 through 9/10.
Dedicated big-game bluewater reel delivering up to 20 lb of sealed carbon drag with very high backing capacity.
Big-game reel with a modified full-frame design (palmable lower half) sharing the 6000 series' sealed carbon drag.
Big-game saltwater reel with a two-stage dual-drag control that presets low-end resistance and dials up high-end fish-fighting power.
Fully-machined, cold-forged saltwater flagship that superseded the 6000 series, with a sealed carbon SCS drag and one-revolution knob.
Long-running, highly-ported big-game saltwater reel built around a sealed, numbered one-revolution disc drag.
Machined large-arbor value series that brought Sage's sealed carbon 'Floating Tripod' drag to a mid price.
Fully-machined, smaller-diameter sealed carbon reel launched alongside the die-cast 2200.
Entry-level die-cast, CNC-finished large-arbor reel with a sealed carbon drag; the 7/8 and 9/10 sizes are spey-friendly.
Machined-spool evolution of the 2500 series using a compact 3:1 geared graphite disc drag.
Larger-diameter concave-spool series with a sealed 3:1 graphite disc drag covering trout through light salt and switch.
Earlier saltwater disc-drag series spanning 4wt through 12wt with a narrower classic spool.
The 2001 reel that introduced Sage's sealed disc drag ('3000 Drag'), in three D-suffixed sizes.
Fully machined large-arbor flagship with the sealed, waterproof Power Matrix carbon-disc drag; each size is a named model keyed to its line class.
Traditional-look machined flagship, individually numbered and supplied in a leather case, using the sealed Power Matrix carbon-disc drag.
Machined spool on a die-cast housing with a V-shaped arbor and a sealed drag rated for fresh and salt water.
Machined mid-tier workhorse built on three frames — FW (featherweight), LW (lightweight) and HD (heavy duty) — all with the sealed Power Matrix drag; later succeeded by the G5.
Entry-level die-cast large-arbor reel that keeps the sealed Power Matrix drag of the machined models.

models.use: all-around trout
half-size heavy, quick loading
long-head versatile
value all-around
all-around trout floating line for medium-to-long casts
models.note: Versatile all-around trout line with a long head and back taper for distance and mending, with SlickCast and a MaxFloat tip.

models.use: all-around trout floating line built a full size heavy
models.note: Front-loaded line a full size heavier than standard with a short head, ideal for fast rods and turning over nymph rigs or big dries.

models.use: general-purpose trout floating line with a shorter head
models.note: Easy-casting all-purpose line for nymphs, dries, streamers and soft hackles with a shorter head for smaller rivers and lakes.

models.use: delicate dry-fly and presentation floating line
models.note: Long, fine front taper and long head for the most delicate presentations and precise casts to rising fish; offered in WF and DT.

models.use: delicate presentation trout floating line for small flies
models.note: Light-touch line with a long fine front taper and high-floating tip for quiet pickups; offered in WF and DT.

models.use: mid-priced all-purpose trout floating line
models.note: All-purpose trout line with a head slightly heavier than standard and a slick self-lubricating coating for easy loading.

models.use: entry-level all-around trout floating line
models.note: Value line with a slightly heavier, shorter head to optimize rod performance, on a supple memory-free core with a welded loop.

models.use: true-to-weight floating line for bamboo, glass and classic rods
models.note: Built to industry line standard (not over-weighted) with a short front taper and compact head to load classic slow-action rods; WF and DT.

models.use: small-creek floating line for short accurate casts
models.note: Short 30-foot front-loaded head that loads at 10–15 feet, for short rods and tight loops, with SlickCast and a MaxFloat tip.

models.use: FIPS-Mouche competition-legal European nymphing line
models.note: Ultra-thin, low-stretch nymphing line with a high-visibility tip for strike detection, rated for 2–5wt rods.

models.use: tropical saltwater flats floating line for bonefish
models.note: RIO's bonefish-specific floating taper on low-memory DirectCore with a long head for efficient flats casts.

models.use: tropical saltwater flats floating line for permit
models.note: Floating-only permit taper on DirectCore with SlickCast for soft crab-pattern presentations while loading powerful rods.

models.use: tropical saltwater floating line for tarpon
models.note: Tarpon-specific floating taper with a mid-length head and powerful front taper.

models.use: multi-purpose tropical saltwater flats line
models.note: RIO's flagship Elite flats line covering 6–12wt in Floating, Float/Intermediate StealthTip and Full Intermediate densities on low-stretch DirectCore.

models.use: inshore saltwater floating line for redfish
models.note: Premier Redfish floating line built to turn over crab, shrimp and baitfish patterns inshore.

models.use: general-purpose tropical saltwater line
models.note: All-around tropical saltwater family offered floating, floating with a 10ft clear intermediate tip, and full intermediate with a clear tip.

models.use: saltwater/predator shooting-head streamer line
models.note: Short, aggressive integrated shooting head; sinking versions are multi-density compound heads (S3 through S7) across the weight range.

models.use: saltwater striped bass line
models.note: Quick-loading 30ft bullet head for big flies in wind; offered floating, clear intermediate, and 30ft sinking heads.

models.use: coldwater saltwater striped bass line
models.note: Entry-level fully clear intermediate striper line sinking ~1.5–2 IPS with a quick-loading weight-forward taper.

models.use: predator/streamer multi-density line
models.note: Built on ConnectCore Plus with a short powerful front taper; offered Floating plus Float/Hover/Intermediate and Float/Sink sink-tip densities.

models.use: powerful big-fly streamer line
models.note: Front-loaded, heavier-than-standard 40ft head on ConnectCore; offered floating plus 3D and 4D multi-density sink-tip versions.

models.use: floating streamer line with a short sink tip
models.note: Floating ConnectCore line with an aggressive front taper and a short 10ft sinking tip (intermediate or Type 6).

models.use: floating line with a 15ft sinking tip for streamers
models.note: ConnectCore floating body with a 15ft tapered sinking tip; offered F/S3 and F/S6 (Type 3/6) densities.

models.use: two-handed Skagit shooting head, floating
models.note: Rear-loaded floating Skagit head built to deliver heavy sink tips and big flies with deep rod loading.

models.use: two-handed Skagit shooting head, floating
models.note: Compact, powerful floating Skagit head for the heaviest tips and largest flies.

models.use: two-handed Scandi head with interchangeable tips
models.note: Floating 23ft Scandi body plus four 10ft interchangeable tips for a 33ft total head, for rods under 13ft.

models.use: single-hand spey / roll-cast full line, floating
models.note: Elite full line with a rear-weighted head for roll and single-handed Spey casts in tight quarters.

models.use: flowing-water river line for overhand and underhand casting
models.note: All-round river line with a two-tone head and an ~8.2 m head, offered in floating and clear-intermediate densities.

models.use: short-head floating line for small streams and tight quarters
models.note: Shorter ~7 m head version of the Stream for close-range command in confined water using overhand or underhand casts.

models.use: all-round single-hand line from dry fly to streamers
models.note: Medium-head (~9 m) all-purpose line offered as a floater and as a Hover (neutral density) that sinks under 2.5 cm/sec to beat surface chop.

models.use: coastal and estuary line for sea trout and bass
models.note: Compact 12 m clear intermediate head for stealth and distance in wind, with a light running line for coastal sea-trout work.

models.use: windy-condition floating line for bulky flies, bombers and poppers
models.note: Compact front taper with a long rear taper to turn over wind-resistant flies with control in harsh conditions.

models.use: long-taper presentation line that doubles for overhead and single-hand spey
models.note: Long front and back tapers for smooth presentation and easy loading on overhead and single-hand spey casts.

models.use: lake line for long casts with long leaders
models.note: Compact ~10.4 m head for distance and delicate long-leader presentation on stillwaters and big rivers.

models.use: short aggressive-taper line for fast rods, wind and tight spaces
models.note: Short front-loaded head that loads fast rods quickly for pick-up-and-shoot casting with big flies in wind.

models.use: medium-to-long-distance presentation line with a handling zone
models.note: 12.2 m head plus a 3 m handling zone for overhead and spey/roll casts at range.

models.use: short-head floating line for modern fast rods and Scandinavian casting
models.note: Mid-tier single-hand line numbered by its 8.5 m head length, built for overhand and underhand casting.
RIO's standard general-purpose copolymer nylon trout leader with a powerful butt for reliable turnover.
RIO's strongest nylon trout leader, up to 20% higher tensile strength than standard Powerflex without sacrificing suppleness.
Very soft, supple copolymer nylon leader for delicate dry-fly and soft-hackle presentations.
100% fluorocarbon trout leader for near-invisible presentations in clear water and to spooky fish.
100% fluorocarbon saltwater/flats leader (bonefish, permit, general salt) sized by pound test with a long turnover butt.
Tough, abrasion-resistant medium-stiff nylon general-purpose saltwater leader that turns over big patterns in wind.
10' tarpon leader with a heavy turnover butt, class tippet, and fluorocarbon bite tippet, designed with Florida Keys guides; coded by class tippet.
12' fully tapered tarpon leader ending in a 2' fluorocarbon shock tippet; coded by class tippet.
Stiffer co-polymer nylon leader with a thick butt and glacial-green non-glare finish for big steelhead and salmon flies.
11' tapered nylon leader joined to 22" of 2X two-tone indicator tippet and finished with a tippet ring.
12' tapered butt with 2' of sighter material, finished with a tippet ring for attaching tippet of choice.
RIO's standard high-knot-strength copolymer nylon trout tippet.
High-strength nylon copolymer rated up to ~20% stronger per diameter than standard Powerflex.
Soft, supple nylon trout tippet for delicate presentations with small flies.
Standard 100% fluorocarbon freshwater trout tippet, low-visibility and abrasion resistant.
High-strength 100% fluorocarbon trout tippet (re-engineered as Fluoroflex Strong in 2021, adding 4.5X/5.5X/6.5X half-sizes).
Pound-rated 100% fluorocarbon saltwater tippet (Fluoroflex Saltwater).
Nylon Euro-nymph sighter tippet with alternating high-visibility colors for strike detection.
Felt restricted in some regions

lineWeightSystem.use: Small streams, spooky trout, light dries
Small streams/creeks, panfish, delicate dry fly
Small-medium trout streams, dry fly, light nymphing
Larger trout, smallmouth, wind, light streamers
Bass, steelhead, light saltwater, big streamers
Saltwater flats, salmon, pike, big bass

notes: Rod, line, and reel weights should match (e.g. a 5wt rod takes a 5wt line and a 5/6 reel).

Model codes are brand-specific: Sage encodes weight-length-pieces (690-4 = 6wt 9'0" 4pc); Orvis Helios encodes length-weight with a taper letter (905 D/F = 9'0" 5wt). Specs are normalized into structured fields so the app never parses the code.
Felt-soled boots are restricted/banned in some regions to limit invasive species.
Tippet breaking strengths are nominal and vary by brand/material.
